text: The surgeon performs a mini diagnostic laparoscopy to visually inspect the appendix, which appears normal, and to rule out the presence of a Meckel's diverticulum.
The surgeon targets the meso-appendix defect for ligation using a bipolar device, followed by cutting the cauterized segment with a mega suture cut.
The surgeon uses a 2-0 silk suture to ligate the base of the appendix, securing it before removal.
The surgeon wraps the suture around the base of the appendix and performs a true suture ligation, securing the structure with precise stitching.
The surgeon wraps the suture around the base again to perform a double ligation, securing the tissue with precise knotting technique.
The surgeon prepares to deliver the excised tissue through the port, or places it in an anocatch bag if the tissue is too large for direct port removal.
The surgeon prepares to use a five millimeter anocatch bag for specimen retrieval if necessary.
The surgeon performs a Z-plasty imbrication, using a slip knot technique to achieve the correct tension for proper imbrication of the tissue.
The surgeon converts an air knot to a slip knot by pulling up on the needle side and down on the side that exited the tissue last, ensuring proper tension for the knot.
The surgeon maintains tension on the needle side to convert the slip knot back into a square knot, ensuring secure ligation of the appendix.
